skill.name: hyperframes-creative
skill.description: Creative direction guidance for HyperFrames video projects, including design specs, palettes, typography, narration, beat planning, and visual style decisions.
skill.summary: Plan the look, tone, pacing, and design system for HyperFrames video compositions.
skill.author: heygen-com

security_audit.summary: The static scanner reported many critical and high findings, but most are false positives from design-language keywords, Markdown examples, and harmless words such as SAME. Real concerns remain: local helper scripts can execute npm, Node, and ffmpeg, and the design picker injects generated preview HTML with innerHTML.

medium_findings.title: Local HTML Injection Surface in Design Picker
medium_findings.description: The design picker renders generated preview_html and option data with innerHTML. The reference warns that preview_html must not contain scripts, event handlers, or javascript URLs, so misuse can create local XSS in the picker page.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The innerHTML sinks are direct and the data source is generated HTML. The file itself documents security constraints, which confirms the risk while limiting it to local picker usage.

medium_findings.title: Local Dependency Bootstrap Executes npm and Re-runs Node
medium_findings.description: The package loader can install pinned helper packages into a temporary directory and re-run the current Node process. It uses confirmation, pinned specs, and --ignore-scripts, but it still executes local package-management commands.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command execution is real, but mitigations are visible: pinned package specs, disabled lifecycle scripts, temporary install root, and interactive confirmation unless an explicit environment flag is set.

medium_findings.title: ffmpeg Subprocess Processes User-Supplied Media
medium_findings.description: The audio extraction helper passes a user-provided media path to ffmpeg and writes JSON output. It avoids shell=True, but ffmpeg parsing of untrusted media is still a local processing risk.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The subprocess call is explicit and uses an argument array, reducing injection risk. The remaining concern is local processing of arbitrary media and arbitrary output path selection.

medium_findings.title: External Runtime and Font Dependencies
medium_findings.description: Several HTML assets load GSAP or fonts from external CDNs. These are normal presentation dependencies, but they create network access and supply-chain exposure during preview or rendering.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The hardcoded URLs are direct and include a remote script in caption skins. The risk is bounded because the URLs are common CDN resources and not evidence of exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Static Critical Sensitive Findings Are Keyword Collisions
low_findings.description: The reported Windows SAM database findings include prose such as SAME in comments, not access to Windows credential stores. I did not find evidence of SAM database reads.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited caption line is a normal English comment, and the contrast script uses the word sample in documentation. These contexts do not indicate credential access.

low_findings.title: Static Weak-Crypto and C2 Findings Are Mostly Design Text
low_findings.description: Many high findings are caused by terms inside frame presets, color descriptions, Markdown examples, and visual style labels. No evidence found of cryptographic routines or command-and-control behavior in those cited design files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed examples are creative direction text and JSON-like documentation, not executable cryptography or C2 logic. Some script files still need warnings, which are reported separately.

low_findings.title: Prompt Injection Search Found No Direct Override Text
low_findings.description: A targeted search for common instruction-override phrases found no evidence of embedded prompts telling the evaluator to ignore instructions, skip review, or change risk levels.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The main skill file contains routing guidance for HyperFrames use, not evaluator override language. This does not prove absence across every phrase variant, but no direct evidence was found.

dangerous_patterns.title: innerHTML Assignment With Generated HTML
dangerous_patterns.description: Generated picker data is inserted into DOM nodes with innerHTML, including preview_html content. This pattern requires sanitization or strict generation controls.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The sink is direct and repeated. The intended use is local preview rendering, so the issue is a controlled but real pattern.

dangerous_patterns.title: Synchronous Child Process Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The package loader invokes npm and then re-runs the current Node program with modified environment variables. This is legitimate helper behavior but should be visible to users.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The child process usage is direct and observable. Mitigations reduce exploitability but do not remove the need for review before execution.

dangerous_patterns.title: Python Subprocess Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The audio helper calls ffmpeg through subprocess.run to decode user-selected media. It does not use a shell, but it executes an external binary.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The subprocess call is direct, but arguments are separated and the command is a standard media-processing tool. This supports a moderate rather than high confidence risk.

content.value_statement: Video compositions often become generic when style decisions are improvised. This skill gives agents structured creative direction for polished HyperFrames videos.
actual_capabilities: Routes agents to the right creative references for HyperFrames video work.
Explains how to read and apply frame.md and design.md specifications.
Provides reusable frame presets with palette, type, layout, and caption guidance.
Guides narration pacing, beat planning, and scene density decisions.
Includes helper scripts for contrast checks and audio-reactive data extraction.
Offers an interactive design picker template for selecting a visual direction.
limitations: It does not provide core HyperFrames rendering rules or CLI documentation.
It intentionally defers animation primitives to the hyperframes-animation skill.
Some helper scripts execute local tools and should be reviewed before use.
Generated picker previews must be sanitized before insertion into the HTML template.

use_cases.title: Shape a Product Launch Video
use_cases.description: Choose a frame preset, typography system, palette, and pacing plan before building a HyperFrames promo video.

use_cases.title: Convert Brand Notes Into Video Style
use_cases.description: Turn a design brief into frame-level rules for color, type, density, captions, and visual tone.

use_cases.title: Improve a Draft Composition
use_cases.description: Audit a draft video for generic layout, weak hierarchy, poor caption styling, and missing produced-video details.

prompt_templates.title: Pick a Visual Direction
prompt_templates.prompt: Use hyperframes-creative to choose a visual direction for a 45-second HyperFrames video about my product. Ask for missing brand details first.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Frame Spec
prompt_templates.prompt: Use hyperframes-creative to draft a frame.md design spec from this brand brief. Include colors, typography, layout, elevation, and caption style.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Scene Beats
prompt_templates.prompt: Use hyperframes-creative to plan scene beats, pacing, narration tone, and visual density for a 60-second explainer video.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Composition Direction
prompt_templates.prompt: Use hyperframes-creative to review this HyperFrames draft for design adherence, video-medium density, typography choices, and brand consistency.

output_examples.input: A launch video for a developer productivity tool with a calm but premium tone.
output_examples.output: A frame direction with a restrained palette, code-focused surfaces, measured pacing, caption rules, and scene-by-scene density notes.

output_examples.input: A playful consumer app needs a 30-second social video style.
output_examples.output: A preset recommendation, palette rationale, typography pairing, caption skin choice, and beat outline for a bright social clip.

output_examples.input: A draft composition looks like a static web page.
output_examples.output: A review that identifies missing frame-scale composition, weak foreground metadata, low visual density, and generic layout choices.

best_practices: Read house-style.md and video-composition.md before making non-trivial design decisions.
Use exact brand tokens from frame.md or design.md instead of inventing nearby values.
Validate generated HTML previews and captions before rendering or sharing outputs.
anti_patterns: Do not treat a video frame like a responsive web landing page.
Do not run helper scripts or dependency bootstrapping without reviewing commands first.
Do not inject untrusted preview_html into the design picker without sanitization.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It guides creative direction for HyperFrames videos, including style, type, palettes, captions, narration, and beat planning.

faq.question: Does it render videos by itself?
faq.answer: No. It supports the creative planning layer. Rendering is handled by HyperFrames tooling and related workflow skills.

faq.question: Can it create animation rules?
faq.answer: It covers high-level motion guidance. Atomic animation patterns are delegated to the hyperframes-animation skill.

faq.question: Does it work with Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Are there security considerations?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes local scripts, external font or script URLs, and an HTML picker that uses innerHTML.

faq.question: When should I use it?
faq.answer: Use it before building or revising a HyperFrames video when visual direction and frame design are important.
